LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER WILLIAM OSWALD'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS (CCP 1094.5) IS GRANTED. THE CITY'S AGREEMENT THAT PETITIONER IS 74% DISABLED AS A RESULT OF THE 2006 INJURY TO HIS BACK REQUIRES THAT PETITIONER'S DISABILITY RETIREMENT PERCENTAGE ALSO BE 74% PER SECTION A8.598-3 OF THE SAN FRANCISCO CHARTER NOTWITHSTANDING THAT THE 74% INCLUDES INJURIES TO PARTS OF PETITIONER'S BODY OTHER THAN HIS BACK. MR. GREY SUBMITTED A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. THE COURT GAVE RESPONDENT UNTIL MAY 21, 2012 TO EMAIL THE COURT AT CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG WITH ANY OBJECTIONS TO THE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus (Ccp 1094.5)
Off Calendar May-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER WILLIAM OSWALD'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS (CCP 1094.5).

Appearances: John J. Grey (457.6444) for petitioner and moving party; Rebecca Liu of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3870) for respondent and opposing party.

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. The City's agreement that petitioner is 74% disabled as a result of the 2006 injury to his back requires that petitioner's disability retirement percentage also be 74% per section A8.598-3 of the San Francisco Charter notwithstanding that the 74% includes injuries to parts of petitioner's body other than his back. Mr. Grey submitted a proposed form of order. The court gave respondent until May 21, 2012 to email the court at contestdept302tr@sftc.org with any objections to the proposed order. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
